revision.comment: 新しいページ：下石橋愛宕塚古墳（栃木県下野市にあった古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 |画像 = |別名 = |所属 = しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域） |所在地 = [[栃木県]][[下野市]]下石橋 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=下石橋愛宕塚古墳}} |形状 = [[帆立貝形古墳]]（下野型古墳） |規模 = 墳丘長84m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 馬具・[[須恵器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = なし |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = 墳丘はほぼ非現存 |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=下石橋
愛宕塚古墳|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=46.20|lon_deg=139|lon_min=51|lon_sec=55.70|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南南西.png|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''下石橋愛宕塚古墳'''（しもいしばしあたごづかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[下野市]]下石橋にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。しもつけ古墳群（うち石橋・薬師寺地域）を構成した古墳の1つ。現在では墳丘はほぼ失われている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域）一覧 !古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |[[横塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長52m||6c後半||（消滅） |- |[[御鷲山古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長83m||6c後半||なし |- |'''下石橋愛宕塚古墳'''||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長84m||6c末||（消滅） |- |[[多功大塚山古墳]]||[[方墳]]||一辺54m||7c中||町史跡 |}
[[ファイル:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館展示。}}]] 栃木県南部、姿川と田川に挟まれた台地上に築造された古墳である。[[東北本線]]・[[東北新幹線]]の建設工事に伴って現在では墳丘のほとんどが失われており、そのうち東北新幹線の工事の際に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形で、前方部を南方向に向けた。墳丘は3段築成で、1段目は帆立貝形であるが、2・3段目は円形である。特に1段目は平坦な基壇状を呈し、下野地域特有の「下野型古墳」の特徴を示す。墳丘表面で[[埴輪]]は認められていないが、[[須恵器]]大甕が検出されている{{Sfn|下石橋愛宕塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘周囲には周溝が巡らされ、周溝を含めた古墳全体としては約112メートルにおよぶ[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 下石橋愛宕塚古墳]（下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム）。。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口した。玄室・前室・羨道からなる複室構造の石室と見られ、玄室・前室には[[凝灰岩]]が使用され、羨道は川原石の小口積みによって構築される。石室内からは、副葬品として馬具（金銅製の鞍金具、鉄地金銅張の轡・辻金具・鏡板・杏葉・大型雲珠・鞖・鉸具）などが検出されている{{Sfn|下石橋愛宕塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]末頃と推定される。 現在では石室石材の凝灰岩が愛宕神社に移設されている。 === 来歴 === * [[明治]]期、[[東北本線]]建設工事に伴う墳丘分断。 * [[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）、[[東北新幹線]]建設工事に伴う発掘調査（栃木県教育委員会、[[1973年]]に報告書刊行）。 == 関連施設 == * しもつけ風土記の丘資料館（下野市国分寺） - 下石橋愛宕塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（石橋町教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大金宣亮|year=1989|chapter=愛宕塚古墳 > 下石橋愛宕塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|下石橋愛宕塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1973|chapter=|title=下石橋愛宕塚古墳（栃木県埋蔵文化財報告書 第9集）|publisher=栃木県教育委員会・日本国有鉄道|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=中山真理|title=下石橋愛宕塚古墳出土の須恵器大甕について(1)|date=2018|journal=栃木県立博物館研究紀要 人文|publisher=栃木県立博物館|volume=|number=35|pages=58-53|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=中山真理|title=下石橋愛宕塚古墳出土の須恵器大甕について(2)|date=2020|journal=栃木県立博物館研究紀要 人文|publisher=栃木県立博物館|volume=|number=37|pages=56-49|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 下石橋愛宕塚古墳] - 下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム {{DEFAULTSORT:しもいしはしあたこつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]]

分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E}} | label1=(横塚) | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西北西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E}} | label2=御鷲山 | label-pos2=left | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E}} | label3=下石橋愛宕塚 | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=帆立貝形古墳-南南西.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|25|16.42|N|139|52|15.73|E}} | label4=多功大塚山 | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=方墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|24|7.05|N|139|52|33.94|E}} | label5=下野薬師寺跡 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=Red pog.svg | mark-size5=12 }} '''下石橋愛宕塚古墳'''（しもいしばしあたごづかこふん）は、[[栃木県]][[下野市]]下石橋にあった[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝形古墳]]。しもつけ古墳群（うち石橋・薬師寺地域）を構成した古墳の1つ。現在では墳丘はほぼ失われている。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+しもつけ古墳群（石橋・薬師寺地域）一覧 !古墳名!!形状!!規模!!築造時期!!史跡指定 |- |[[横塚古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長52m||6c後半||（消滅） |- |[[御鷲山古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長83m||6c後半||なし |- |'''下石橋愛宕塚古墳'''||[[帆立貝形古墳]]||墳丘長84m||6c末||（消滅） |- |[[多功大塚山古墳]]||[[方墳]]||一辺54m||7c中||町史跡 |}
[[ファイル:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館展示。}}]] 栃木県南部、姿川と田川に挟まれた台地上に築造された古墳である。[[東北本線]]・[[東北新幹線]]の建設工事に伴って現在では墳丘のほとんどが失われており、そのうち東北新幹線の工事の際に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方部が短小な帆立貝形の前方後円形で、前方部を南方向に向けた。墳丘は3段築成で、1段目は帆立貝形であるが、2・3段目は円形である。特に1段目は平坦な基壇状を呈し、下野地域特有の「下野型古墳」の特徴を示す。墳丘表面で[[埴輪]]は認められていないが、[[須恵器]]大甕が検出されている{{Sfn|下石橋愛宕塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。墳丘周囲には周溝が巡らされ、周溝を含めた古墳全体としては約112メートルにおよぶ[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 下石橋愛宕塚古墳]（下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム）。。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口した。玄室・前室・羨道からなる複室構造の石室と見られ、玄室・前室には[[凝灰岩]]が使用され、羨道は川原石の小口積みによって構築される。石室内からは、副葬品として馬具（金銅製の鞍金具、鉄地金銅張の轡・辻金具・鏡板・杏葉・大型雲珠・鞖・鉸具）などが検出されている{{Sfn|下石橋愛宕塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}。築造時期は[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]末頃と推定される。 現在では石室石材の凝灰岩が愛宕神社に移設されている。 === 来歴 === * [[明治]]期、[[東北本線]]建設工事に伴う墳丘分断。 * [[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）、[[東北新幹線]]建設工事に伴う発掘調査（栃木県教育委員会、[[1973年]]に報告書刊行）。 == 関連施設 == * しもつけ風土記の丘資料館（下野市国分寺） - 下石橋愛宕塚古墳の出土品等を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（石橋町教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=大金宣亮|year=1989|chapter=愛宕塚古墳 > 下石橋愛宕塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|下石橋愛宕塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1973|chapter=|title=下石橋愛宕塚古墳（栃木県埋蔵文化財報告書 第9集）|publisher=栃木県教育委員会・日本国有鉄道|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=中山真理|title=下石橋愛宕塚古墳出土の須恵器大甕について(1)|date=2018|journal=栃木県立博物館研究紀要 人文|publisher=栃木県立博物館|volume=|number=35|pages=58-53|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=中山真理|title=下石橋愛宕塚古墳出土の須恵器大甕について(2)|date=2020|journal=栃木県立博物館研究紀要 人文|publisher=栃木県立博物館|volume=|number=37|pages=56-49|url=|naid=|doi=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 下石橋愛宕塚古墳] - 下野市文化財バーチャルミュージアム {{DEFAULTSORT:しもいしはしあたこつかこふん}} [[Category:栃木県の古墳]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Kofun of Japan |Name = Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun |Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}} {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}} Shimoishibashi |Coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Shimotsuke-style) |Size = Mound length: 84m |Burial Facilities = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |Accompanying Burial Mounds = None |Era = Late 6th century |Burial = Unknown |Historic Designation = None |Image = |Additional Info = The mound is almost completely destroyed. |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Shimoishibashi
Atagozuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=46.20|lon_deg=139|lon_min=51|lon_sec=55.70|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|25|18|N|139|51|42|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 250| height =320 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi/Yakushiji Area)}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E}} | label1=(Yokozuka) | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西北西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E}} | label2=Owashiyama | label-pos2=left | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E}} | label3=Shimoishibashi Atagozuka | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=帆立貝形古墳-南南西.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|25|16.42|N|139|52|15.73|E}} | label4=Tako Ōtsukayama | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=方墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|24|7.05|N|139|52|33.94|E}} | label5=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=Red pog.svg | mark-size5=12 }} '''Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a scallop-shaped kofun and was part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}}, specifically the Ishibashi/Yakushiji area. The mound is now almost entirely lost. == Overview == {{thumb|right|250px|Artifacts from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun, displayed at Shimotsuke Fudoki no Oka Museum}} The kofun was constructed on a plateau between the Sugata River and the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the Tōhoku Shinkansen construction. The mound was a scallop-shaped tumulus, with a short rectangular front extending southward. It featured a three-tiered construction, with the first tier in the scallop shape and the second and third tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Virtual Museum The first tier had a flat, platform-like appearance, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were found on the mound surface, but a large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jar was uncovered. A moat surrounded the mound, giving the entire site, including the moat, a total length of approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southward. It consisted of a multi-room structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passageway. The chambers were constructed with tuff, while the passageway was built using small river stones in a stacked arrangement. Artifacts recovered from the chamber include horse fittings (gilded bronze saddle ornaments, iron and gilded bronze bridles, fittings, saddle plates, and pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the end of the 6th century. The tuff stones from the chamber are now relocated to the nearby Atago Shrine. == History == * {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}: The mound was partially destroyed during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 47): Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen (reported in 1973 by the Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki no Oka Museum (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) - Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in writing this article}} * Explanatory plaque (Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobuyasu Ōgane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|Additional sources not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1973|chapter=|title=Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (Tochigi Prefectural Archaeological Reports, No. 9)|publisher=Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education and Japanese National Railways|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Mari Nakayama|title=On Sue Ware Jar Excavated from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (1)|date=2018|journal=Tochigi Prefectural Museum Bulletin of Humanities|publisher=Tochigi Prefectural Museum|volume=|number=35|pages=58-53|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Mari Nakayama|title=On Sue Ware Jar Excavated from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (2)|date=2020|journal=Tochigi Prefectural Museum Bulletin of Humanities|publisher=Tochigi Prefectural Museum|volume=|number=37|pages=56-49|ref=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Virtual Museum [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke, Tochigi]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Kofun of Japan |Name = Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun |Location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}} {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}} Shimoishibashi |Coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun}} |Type = Scallop-shaped Kofun (Shimotsuke-style) |Size = Mound length: 84m |Burial Facilities = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |Artifacts = Horse fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} |Accompanying Burial Mounds = None |Era = Late 6th century |Burial = Unknown |Historic Designation = None |Image = |Additional Info = The mound is almost completely destroyed. |Map2 = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Shimoishibashi
Atagozuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=46.20|lon_deg=139|lon_min=51|lon_sec=55.70|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|25|18|N|139|51|42|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 250| height =320 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi/Yakushiji Area)}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E}} | label1=(Yokozuka) | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西北西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E}} | label2=Owashiyama | label-pos2=left | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E}} | label3=Shimoishibashi Atagozuka | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=帆立貝形古墳-南南西.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|25|16.42|N|139|52|15.73|E}} | label4=Tako Ōtsukayama | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=方墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|24|7.05|N|139|52|33.94|E}} | label5=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=Red pog.svg | mark-size5=12 }} '''Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a scallop-shaped kofun and was part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}}, specifically the Ishibashi/Yakushiji area. The mound is now almost entirely lost. == Overview == {{thumb|right|250px|Artifacts from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun, displayed at Shimotsuke Fudoki no Oka Museum}} The kofun was constructed on a plateau between the Sugata River and the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the Tōhoku Shinkansen construction. The mound was a scallop-shaped tumulus, with a short rectangular front extending southward. It featured a three-tiered construction, with the first tier in the scallop shape and the second and third tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Virtual Museum The first tier had a flat, platform-like appearance, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were found on the mound surface, but a large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jar was uncovered. A moat surrounded the mound, giving the entire site, including the moat, a total length of approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southward. It consisted of a multi-room structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passageway. The chambers were constructed with tuff, while the passageway was built using small river stones in a stacked arrangement. Artifacts recovered from the chamber include horse fittings (gilded bronze saddle ornaments, iron and gilded bronze bridles, fittings, saddle plates, and pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the end of the 6th century. The tuff stones from the chamber are now relocated to the nearby Atago Shrine. == History == * {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}: The mound was partially destroyed during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 47): Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen (reported in 1973 by the Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki no Oka Museum (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) - Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in writing this article}} * Explanatory plaque (Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobuyasu Ōgane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofun|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|Additional sources not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1973|chapter=|title=Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (Tochigi Prefectural Archaeological Reports, No. 9)|publisher=Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education and Japanese National Railways|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Mari Nakayama|title=On Sue Ware Jar Excavated from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (1)|date=2018|journal=Tochigi Prefectural Museum Bulletin of Humanities|publisher=Tochigi Prefectural Museum|volume=|number=35|pages=58-53|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Mari Nakayama|title=On Sue Ware Jar Excavated from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (2)|date=2020|journal=Tochigi Prefectural Museum Bulletin of Humanities|publisher=Tochigi Prefectural Museum|volume=|number=37|pages=56-49|ref=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Virtual Museum [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke, Tochigi]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]]

Atagozuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=46.20|lon_deg=139|lon_min=51|lon_sec=55.70|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|25|18|N|139|51|42|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 250| height =320 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi/Yakushiji Area)}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E}} | label1=(Yokozuka) | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西北西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E}} | label2=Owashiyama | label-pos2=left | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E}} | label3=Shimoishibashi Atagozuka | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=帆立貝形古墳-南南西.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|25|16.42|N|139|52|15.73|E}} | label4=Tako Ōtsukayama | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=方墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|24|7.05|N|139|52|33.94|E}} | label5=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=Red pog.svg | mark-size5=12 }} '''Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a scallop-shaped kofun and was part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}}, specifically the Ishibashi/Yakushiji area. The mound is now almost entirely lost. == Overview == {{thumb|right|250px|Artifacts from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun, displayed at Shimotsuke Fudoki no Oka Museum}} The kofun was constructed on a plateau between the Sugata River and the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the Tōhoku Shinkansen construction. The mound was a scallop-shaped tumulus, with a short rectangular front extending southward. It featured a three-tiered construction, with the first tier in the scallop shape and the second and third tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Virtual Museum The first tier had a flat, platform-like appearance, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were found on the mound surface, but a large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jar was uncovered. A moat surrounded the mound, giving the entire site, including the moat, a total length of approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southward. It consisted of a multi-room structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passageway. The chambers were constructed with tuff, while the passageway was built using small river stones in a stacked arrangement. Artifacts recovered from the chamber include horse fittings (gilded bronze saddle ornaments, iron and gilded bronze bridles, fittings, saddle plates, and pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the end of the 6th century. The tuff stones from the chamber are now relocated to the nearby Atago Shrine. == History == * {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}: The mound was partially destroyed during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 47): Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen (reported in 1973 by the Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki no Oka Museum (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) - Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in writing this article}} * Explanatory plaque (Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobuyasu Ōgane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|Additional sources not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1973|chapter=|title=Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (Tochigi Prefectural Archaeological Reports, No. 9)|publisher=Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education and Japanese National Railways|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Mari Nakayama|title=On Sue Ware Jar Excavated from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (1)|date=2018|journal=Tochigi Prefectural Museum Bulletin of Humanities|publisher=Tochigi Prefectural Museum|volume=|number=35|pages=58-53|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Mari Nakayama|title=On Sue Ware Jar Excavated from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (2)|date=2020|journal=Tochigi Prefectural Museum Bulletin of Humanities|publisher=Tochigi Prefectural Museum|volume=|number=37|pages=56-49|ref=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Atagozuka Kofun|lat_deg=36|lat_min=25|lat_sec=46.20|lon_deg=139|lon_min=51|lon_sec=55.70|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} {{OSM location map | coord = {{Coord|36|25|18|N|139|51|42|E}} | zoom = 13 | float = right | width = 250| height =320 | caption = {{center|Distribution Map of Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi/Yakushiji Area)}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|26|30.90|N|139|51|17.55|E}} | label1=(Yokozuka) | label-pos1=left | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-西北西.svg | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|24|19.07|N|139|52|36.88|E}} | label2=Owashiyama | label-pos2=left | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-西.svg | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E}} | label3=Shimoishibashi Atagozuka | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=帆立貝形古墳-南南西.svg | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|25|16.42|N|139|52|15.73|E}} | label4=Tako Ōtsukayama | label-pos4=top | label-color4=black | mark4=方墳.svg | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|24|7.05|N|139|52|33.94|E}} | label5=Shimotsuke Yakushiji Ruins | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=Red pog.svg | mark-size5=12 }} '''Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}. It is a scallop-shaped kofun and was part of the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}}, specifically the Ishibashi/Yakushiji area. The mound is now almost entirely lost. == Overview == {{thumb|right|250px|Artifacts from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun, displayed at Shimotsuke Fudoki no Oka Museum}} The kofun was constructed on a plateau between the Sugata River and the Tagawa River in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the Tōhoku Shinkansen construction. The mound was a scallop-shaped tumulus, with a short rectangular front extending southward. It featured a three-tiered construction, with the first tier in the scallop shape and the second and third tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Virtual Museum The first tier had a flat, platform-like appearance, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." No {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} were found on the mound surface, but a large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jar was uncovered. A moat surrounded the mound, giving the entire site, including the moat, a total length of approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southward. It consisted of a multi-room structure with a main chamber, an antechamber, and a passageway. The chambers were constructed with tuff, while the passageway was built using small river stones in a stacked arrangement. Artifacts recovered from the chamber include horse fittings (gilded bronze saddle ornaments, iron and gilded bronze bridles, fittings, saddle plates, and pendants). The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the end of the 6th century. The tuff stones from the chamber are now relocated to the nearby Atago Shrine. == History == * {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}: The mound was partially destroyed during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 47): Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen (reported in 1973 by the Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education). == Related Facilities == * Shimotsuke Fudoki no Oka Museum (Shimotsuke City Kokubunji) - Houses and displays artifacts from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} {{Footnotes Help}} == Sources == {{small|Sources used in writing this article}} * Explanatory plaque (Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobuyasu Ōgane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Further Reading == {{small|Additional sources not used in this article}} * {{Cite book|author=|year=1973|chapter=|title=Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (Tochigi Prefectural Archaeological Reports, No. 9)|publisher=Tochigi Prefectural Board of Education and Japanese National Railways|isbn=|page=|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Mari Nakayama|title=On Sue Ware Jar Excavated from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (1)|date=2018|journal=Tochigi Prefectural Museum Bulletin of Humanities|publisher=Tochigi Prefectural Museum|volume=|number=35|pages=58-53|ref=}} * {{Cite journal|author=Mari Nakayama|title=On Sue Ware Jar Excavated from Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun (2)|date=2020|journal=Tochigi Prefectural Museum Bulletin of Humanities|publisher=Tochigi Prefectural Museum|volume=|number=37|pages=56-49|ref=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoishibashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Heritage Virtual Museum [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke, Tochigi]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped Kofun]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]]

{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. [[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. [[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]]

revision.comment: linking [[Square Kofun]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:方墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:方墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]]
