revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. [[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==

Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Bump 1-20 (2)]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}, Japan. It was one of the burial mounds forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|Artifacts from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum.
