revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | location = {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Shimotsuke|ja|下野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke|qq=}}, {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It was one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwashi Yama Kofun|qq=}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhoku Main Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhoku Shinkansen|qq=}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|version=99996049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]] [[en:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]]

It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|version=99996049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]] [[en:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]]

Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==

[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the {{ill|Tōhoku Main Line|ja|東北本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhoku Main Line|qq=}} and {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|ja|東北新幹線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōhoku Shinkansen|qq=}}, most of the mound has been destroyed. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|version=99996049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]] [[en:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]]

The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|version=99996049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]] [[en:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]]

{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Shimotsuke, Tochigi|Shimotsuke]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Shimotsuke, Tochigi|Shimotsuke]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It was one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwashi Yama Kofun|qq=}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] and [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]], most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|version=99996049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]] [[en:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:栃木県の古墳]]

Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|version=99996049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Shimotsuke, Tochigi|Shimotsuke]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Shimotsuke, Tochigi|Shimotsuke]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It was one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwashi Yama Kofun|qq=}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|qq=}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] and [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]], most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|version=99996049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]]

{{Infobox historic site | name = Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun | native_name = 下石橋愛宕塚古墳 | native_language = ja | image = | caption = | type = {{ill|Scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | location = [[:en:Shimotsuke, Tochigi|Shimotsuke]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{coord|36|25|46.20|N|139|51|55.70|E|display=inline,title}} | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | designation1 = }} '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' (下石橋愛宕塚古墳) was a {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Shimotsuke, Tochigi|Shimotsuke]], [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]], Japan. It was one of the Kofuns forming the {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Ishibashi-Yakushiji region). Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster (Ishibashi-Yakushiji Region) ! Name !! Shape !! Size !! Construction Period !! Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwashi Yama Kofun|qq=}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] and [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]], most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|version=99996049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]] [[Category:下野市の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]]

Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==

Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwashi Yama Kofun|qq=}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] and [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]], most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|version=99996049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]] [[Category:Municipal history of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Today, most of the mound has been lost. == Overview ==

Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwashi Yama Kofun|qq=}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] and [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]], most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|version=99996049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun]] [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]] [[Category:Municipal history of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwashi Yama Kofun|qq=}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] and [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]], most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwashi Yama Kofun|qq=}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] and [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]], most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwashi Yama Kofun|qq=}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] and [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]], most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135493142}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwashi Yama Kofun|qq=}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] and [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]], most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135493142}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135491515}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|version=99996049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]] [[Category:Municipal history of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwashi Yama Kofun|qq=}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] and [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]], most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493142}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|qq=|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491515}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|version=99996049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]] [[Category:Municipal history of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q113771626}}

Historical Designation |- | {{ill|Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotsuka Kofun|lt=Yokotzuka Kofun|ja|横塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokotzuka Kofun|qq=}} || [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] || Mound length 52 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Owashiyama Kofun|lt=Miwashi Yama Kofun|ja|御鷲山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwashi Yama Kofun|qq=}} || Keyhole-shaped || Mound length 83 m || Late 6th century || None |- | '''Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun''' || Scallop-shaped || Mound length 84 m || Late 6th century || (Destroyed) |- | {{ill|Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja|多功大塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuno Otsukayama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} || {{ill|Square-shaped kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square-shaped kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} || 54 m on each side || Mid-7th century || Town historical site |}
[[File:下石橋愛宕塚古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|250px|right|burial goods from the site (on display at the Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum)]] The kofun was built on a plateau between the Sugata and Tagawa Rivers in southern Tochigi Prefecture. Due to the construction of the [[:en:Tōhoku Main Line|Tōhoku Main Line]] and [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]], most of the mound has been destroyed. Excavations were conducted during the construction of the Shinkansen. The mound was scallop-shaped, with a short rectangular front projecting southward. It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main Line. * 1972 (Shōwa 47): Excavation conducted during the construction of the Tōhoku Shinkansen by the Tochigi Prefecture Board of Education, with a report published in 1973. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja|しもつけ風土記の丘資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Fudoki-no-Oka Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135493142}} (Shimotsuke City) – Preserves and displays artifacts from Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Marker (Installed by Ishibashi Town Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Nobutsuki Okane|year=1989|chapter=Atagozuka Kofun > Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun|title={{ill|Japanese Kofun Dictionary|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Kofun Dictionary|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Publishing|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4490102607}} == See Also == * {{ill|Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja|しもつけ古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135491515}} == External Links == * [http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] – Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. {{translated page|ja|下石橋愛宕塚古墳|version=99996049|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:下石橋愛宕塚古墳]] [[Category:Municipal history of Shimotsuke]] [[Category:Kofun of Tochigi prefecture]] {{wikidata link|Q113771626}}

It was constructed in three tiers, with the first tier being scallop-shaped and the upper tiers circular.[http://www.shimotsuke-bunkazai.com/culturalassets.php?id=33 Shimoiwabashi Atagozuka Kofun] - Shimotsuke Cultural Assets Virtual Museum. The base was flat, characteristic of the "Shimotsuke-style kofun." Although no haniwa were found on the mound, large [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] jars were unearthed. The total length of the mound, including the surrounding moat, was approximately 112 meters. The burial facility was a {{ill|corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. The chamber had multiple sections (main chamber, antechamber, and passageway). The main and antechamber were constructed using tuff blocks, while the passageway was built with river stones. Excavations revealed grave goods such as horse trappings, including gold-plated saddle fittings, bronze bits, and other decorated items. The kofun is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century. Today, tuff blocks from the stone chamber have been relocated to a nearby Atago Shrine. == Excavation History == * Late 19th century: Partial destruction during the construction of the Tōhoku Main
